description_long: This is a traditional wooden windmill with a log-cabin style base and a large, multi-bladed rotor mounted at the front. The structure is constructed from horizontally stacked logs forming the main body, topped with a wooden gabled roof. The windmill features six long, lattice-style blades radiating from a central hub, each made from wooden slats and beams. A small rectangular window is visible on the front face, suggesting an interior space that could be accessed. The wood appears weathered, giving the windmill a rustic, historical appearance typical of rural or park settings. The overall design is functional and robust, suitable for use as a landmark or decorative amenity in an urban park or open space.
description: A rustic wooden windmill with a log-cabin base, gabled roof, and six large lattice-style blades. The weathered wood and small window add to its historical charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the six-bladed rotor, log-cabin base, gabled roof, and a small window.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the log structure and the depth of the roof, with blades seen edge-on.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the gabled roof and log-cabin structure, with the rotor blades extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the log construction and the profile of the windmill blades.
